behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is parser validation/message logic within one module. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if error text is assembled by a higher API layer, Unit could miss formatting regressions. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Integration): behavior text says "parser rejects invalid expressions" (parser-local contract), so wiring multiple modules is not required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text contains only cron syntax/value validation and error messaging, with no real infra or AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Call parser directly with invalid inputs (`13` month, `32` day, malformed tokens) and assert structured error type/code plus message substrings identifying offending field/value.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator computes correct timestamps, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core risk is date-rule algorithm correctness in calculator logic. REAL-INFRA: deterministic time math, no required OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if calculator only works correctly after parser normalization, Unit on calculator alone may miss expression-shape mismatches. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Integration): behavior targets calculator correctness, not parser-calculator handoff; direct schedule-object tests hit the stated failure boundary. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration as minimum because behavior text does not require cross-module interaction to expose rule-computation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Given canonical schedule objects for monthly-last-day and nearest-weekday patterns plus fixed start times, assert next N timestamps exactly match expected calendar outcomes (including month length/leap-year cases).

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules firing within minimum gap and reports conflict windows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real bug often appears at handoff between run-time generation and overlap-window construction (normalization, sorting, boundary inclusivity). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI output, (c) no agentic chain, (d) no LLM-involved orchestration. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: detector may be a pure interval function; then Unit could suffice. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Unit): behavior says "would fire" and "reports conflicting time windows," which implies combining schedule outputs with detector/reporting logic, a module boundary. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text has no real infra dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scheduler components in-process: generate candidate fire times for two cron schedules, run overlap detection with 5-minute threshold, assert detected windows and boundary handling (`exactly 5m`, `4m59s`, multiple collisions) match expected reports.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is typically interaction between schedule computation and timezone/DST resolution policy (skip/shift/first-or-second occurrence). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) behavior text does not require real OS timezone services or subprocesses; in-process tz library + rules is sufficient, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM stage handoff. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if production relies on host tzdata differences, only System could catch environment-specific drift. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up System): behavior text asks for DST correctness semantics, not host-environment behavior under real OS primitives. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because text does not mention system clock, OS tz config, or external services.
behaviors.test_description: In-process integration test using real timezone rules: for a DST-observing zone, compute next runs around spring-forward and fall-back dates and assert configured handling for nonexistent 2:30 and ambiguous 2:30 occurrences.

behaviors.description: When next run is in the past after downtime, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears in orchestration between next-run computation, persisted last-run/now context, and policy evaluator. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) can be tested with injected clock/in-memory store; no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agentic steps, (d) no LLM-involved handoff. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if policy decision logic is isolated and pure, Unit might catch most failures. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Unit): behavior text is engine-level decisioning "computed next run has already passed" plus policy application, which is cross-module state handoff. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because downtime scenario can be reproduced via controlled timestamps without real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scheduler service + policy module + state repository (local/in-memory): set `now` after expected fire time, vary policy (`skip`, `backfill`), and assert resulting queued executions and updated schedule state.
